Identified the team's next hire and contributed to the job description
Established a technical mentorship relationship with at least one team member
Know the regulatory requirements well enough to design compliant systems independently
days90.deliverable: 6-month technical roadmap presentation with business justification, trade-offs, and resource requirements
days90.antipattern: Don't measure success by lines of code written. At Director level, your output is decisions, standards, direction, and people development.

javaVersions.feature: var for local variables, HTTP Client API, String methods (strip, isBlank, lines)
javaVersions.useWhen: Baseline — most enterprise teams are here minimum
Records = immutable data carriers, replaces Lombok @Value
if (obj instanceof String s) — no cast needed
Current enterprise LTS. Sealed classes = exhaustive type hierarchies

javaVersions.feature: Virtual Threads (stable), Sequenced Collections, Pattern Matching (stable)
javaVersions.useWhen: Virtual threads replace reactive for I/O-bound services. Target for new projects.

springAnnotations.use: = @Controller + @ResponseBody. For REST APIs that return JSON/XML.
Stereotype annotations — mark beans for component scanning. @Repository adds exception translation.
Wraps method in a DB transaction. ONLY works on public methods via Spring proxy. rollbackFor for checked exceptions.
Method-level caching. @Cacheable = read-through. @CacheEvict = invalidate. @CachePut = always update.
Run method on a schedule. Use fixedRate, fixedDelay, or cron expression.
Run method in separate thread. Requires @EnableAsync on config class. Returns CompletableFuture.
@Value for single property. @ConfigurationProperties for typed binding of a group of properties.
Method-level security. Requires @EnableMethodSecurity. Uses Spring EL expressions.
Mark method as Kafka consumer. topics, groupId, containerFactory are key attributes.

kafkaBestPractices.why: Wait for all in-sync replicas to acknowledge — no data loss on broker failure
Exactly-once delivery on producer side — deduplicates retried sends
New consumer starts from beginning of topic — safe for event sourcing
Manual offset commit — commit AFTER processing, not before
Batch size per poll — tune based on processing time vs. session timeout
Only read messages from committed transactions — needed for exactly-once
Compress messages — reduces network and storage. LZ4 is fast with good compression ratio
Require 2 replicas in sync before acknowledging — prevents data loss

kubernetesResources.use: Manage a set of identical pods. Handles rolling updates, scaling, rollback.
Internal load balancer for pods. Stable DNS name. ClusterIP = internal only.
Exposes service to internet via cloud load balancer. Creates external IP.
HTTP/HTTPS routing. Route by host/path to different services. TLS termination.
Store non-sensitive config as key-value or files. Mounted as env vars or volumes.
Store sensitive data (base64 encoded). Use external secrets operator for real security.
Auto-scale pods based on CPU/memory/custom metrics.
Ensure minimum pods remain available during voluntary disruptions (drains, updates).
Firewall rules for pods — restrict which pods can talk to which.
Identity for pods — controls what Kubernetes APIs the pod can call.

restApiChecklist: Use nouns for resources, not verbs: /orders not /getOrders
HTTP methods: GET (read), POST (create), PUT (replace), PATCH (partial update), DELETE

Status codes: 200 (OK), 201 (Created), 204 (No Content), 400 (Bad Request), 401 (Unauthorized), 403 (Forbidden), 404 (Not Found), 409 (Conflict), 422 (Unprocessable), 429 (Rate Limited), 500 (Server Error)
Version in URL path: /api/v1/... (avoid header versioning for simplicity)
Pagination: use cursor-based (more efficient) or offset+limit. Always include total count.
Error response: always return structured JSON with code, message, timestamp, traceId
Idempotency header for mutating operations: Idempotency-Key
Never return 200 with an error body — use correct HTTP status codes
Document with OpenAPI 3.0 spec — generated from code, not written by hand
Rate limit headers: X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset
securityChecklist: Authentication: OAuth2 + OIDC with short-lived JWTs (15 min expiry)
Authorization: validate on every request at API gateway + service layer
Never log passwords, tokens, PII, or financial data
Validate and sanitize ALL external inputs (headers, query params, body)
Use parameterized queries / JPA — never string-concatenate SQL
Secrets in Vault/Secrets Manager — never in code, env vars, or config files in repo
CORS: whitelist specific origins, never use *  in production
Dependency scanning: run Snyk/Dependabot on every build — block on critical CVEs
Container scanning: Trivy in CI pipeline before pushing images
Principle of least privilege: service accounts with minimum required permissions only
Audit log every sensitive operation (read AND write) with user, timestamp, data accessed

genAiPatterns.use: LLM answers questions about YOUR data. Embed your documents → find relevant chunks → add to prompt. Use for internal chatbots, code search, documentation Q&A.
Ask LLM to reason step-by-step before answering. Reduces hallucinations on complex reasoning tasks.
LLM decides to call your APIs/functions to get real data. Use for agents that can look up prices, execute trades, query databases.
Cache LLM responses by semantic similarity of questions. 'What is the stock price of Apple?' and 'Apple stock price?' return the same cached response.
Centralized proxy for all LLM calls. Adds: rate limiting, cost tracking, PII redaction, prompt injection detection, model fallback.
For high-stakes decisions (trades, approvals), LLM suggests but human confirms. Prevents autonomous AI actions with irreversible financial consequences.
